title: Internal Consistency as Guide (ICG): Test-Time Self-Correction for Diffusion Models via Cross-Noise-Level Verification
description: We propose a training-free test-time refinement method for diffusion models that uses internal prediction consistency across noise levels as a quality signal. The key insight is that well-generated samples should produce consistent clean-image predictions from different timesteps along the denoising trajectory. By measuring cross-noise-level disagreement, we can identify low-quality samples and regions, then allocate additional compute adaptively for targeted refinement without requiring external reward models or expensive search.
motivation: Current diffusion models treat all samples uniformly with fixed sampling budgets, despite varying sample difficulty. While test-time scaling methods exist, they require external reward models, population-based search, or fine-tuning. We lack a training-free method that leverages the model's own internal signals to guide adaptive compute allocation. This work fills that gap by exploiting the observation that inconsistent predictions across noise levels signal poor generation quality.
proposed_approach: Our ICG method operates in three phases: (1) Standard sampling while tracking Internal Consistency Score (ICS) - the similarity between clean-image predictions derived from different timesteps; (2) Inconsistency-guided local refinement that adds controlled noise and re-denoises regions with low ICS; (3) Verification and iteration until consistency threshold is met. We also develop spatially-adaptive refinement using per-pixel consistency maps for targeted correction.
hypothesis: Cross-noise-level prediction consistency (ICS) correlates with sample quality, and using inconsistency signals to guide test-time refinement improves the quality-efficiency trade-off without requiring external reward models or retraining.
success_criteria: ICS reliably predicts sample quality (correlation > 0.7 with quality metrics)
ICG achieves statistically significant FID improvement over standard sampling with same NFE budget
ICG achieves better FID-NFE Pareto frontier than search-based TTS methods
Method generalizes across model architectures (UNet, DiT) and datasets
expected_contribution: First method to use cross-noise-level consistency for test-time self-correction in image diffusion models
Training-free approach applicable to any pretrained diffusion model without modification
Spatially-adaptive compute allocation for efficient targeted refinement
Demonstration that internal model signals can replace external reward models for test-time scaling
key_differentiators: vs. Consistency Models: We use inconsistency as a test-time diagnostic rather than training objective
vs. Test-Time Scaling (EvoSearch, TTSnap): No external reward model or population search needed
vs. Adaptive Sampling (AdaDiff): We do cross-timestep verification, not just single-step uncertainty
vs. Temporal Self-Consistency in dLLMs: We extend to continuous images with spatial adaptation
experimental_focus: CIFAR-10 for fast prototyping and ablation studies
Comparison with DDIM, DPM-Solver++, EvoSearch, and TTSnap
Evaluation using FID, Precision/Recall, ICS distribution, and NFE efficiency

feasibility.compute: Experiments require 1x A6000 (48GB). Training not needed (training-free method). Evaluation on standard benchmarks (CIFAR-10, ImageNet 64x64) feasible within 8 hours.
feasibility.data: Standard public datasets (CIFAR-10, ImageNet). No new data collection needed.
feasibility.codebase: Can build on diffusers library and existing diffusion model implementations.

similar_works: Consistency Models (Song et al., 2023) - enforces consistency during training
Temporal Self-Consistency in dLLMs (2025) - voting across timesteps for text
EvoSearch/TTSnap (2025) - test-time scaling via search/reward models
AdaDiff (2024) - adaptive early exit based on uncertainty
novelty_assessment.gap_addressed: No existing method uses cross-noise-level inconsistency as a training-free signal for test-time self-correction in continuous image diffusion models with spatially-adaptive refinement.
novelty_assessment.risk_of_scooping: Low - the specific combination of ICS-based quality estimation and targeted test-time refinement is novel and not explored in concurrent work.
